commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is the explicit URL credential/security contract, not a purely mechanical code edit.; Needs attention: No supply-chain changes were found, but the PR changes a gateway credential boundary and explicit URL auth policy still needs maintainer attention before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Keep explicit URL probes out of local SecretRef fail-fast â€” src/commands... (sha=4d1bcaf245be890614ec06cf205c247e25e66d80)
commands.review_summary:
The PR propagates gateway probe auth `failureReason` values through status/probe paths so missing required gateway auth can synthesize a failed probe instead of opening an anonymous WebSocket, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +149, Tests +225. Total +374 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was run in this read-only review. Source inspection clearly shows the latest explicit URL fail-fast branch, and the PR body provides helper-level terminal proof for related cases but not the full latest CLI override path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is the explicit URL credential/security contract, not a purely mechanical code edit.

target.title: fix(gateway): fail fast on missing local probe auth
target.body:
## Summary
- fail fast for local loopback probe/status auth resolution when gateway auth is required but unresolved
- return a local probe failure instead of attempting an anonymous loopback websocket connect
- add focused regression coverage for the local authenticated probe/status paths

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/probe-auth src/commands/status.scan src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather src/commands/gateway-status` -> 4 files, 34 tests pass
- `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean
- `pnpm build` -> clean

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: When gateway auth is required (`gateway.auth.mode = "token"` or `"password"`) but no credentials resolve (env SecretRef missing, no explicit token, no resolvable secret), the local probe path previously opened an anonymous loopback WebSocket and waited for it to time out with a misleading "connect failed". This PR makes the probe short-circuit immediately with a structured `failureReason` naming the missing credential, and leaves `mode: "none"`, `mode: "trusted-proxy"`, undefined auth mode, remote-mode probes, and explicit-URL targets on the existing (non-fail-fast) path.

Real environment tested: live deployed openclaw build on rh-bot.lan, gateway pid 71842, build SHA `23804e610c998a0532e0c90441b130b38e5e8cc8`, version `2026.5.28`, built `2026-05-31T23:35:55Z`. The deployed bundle file `dist/probe-auth-FKOXYPWa.js` already contains the new `resolveLocalProbeFailureReason` / `resolveLocalProbeFailureReasonSync` helpers and the `mode === "local"` + `authMode` guards from this PR. The proof script imports the deployed bundle directly from the live gateway tree on rh-bot and exercises both the sync and async exported entry points the CLI status/scan/probe-run paths call into.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
# On rh-bot.lan, against the live deployed openclaw build
node /tmp/proof-68280-amittell.mjs 2>&1 | tee /tmp/proof-68280-amittell.txt
```

The script imports the deployed bundle directly:

```
import {
  n as resolveGatewayProbeAuthSafe,
  r as resolveGatewayProbeAuthSafeWithSecretInputs,
} from "/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/probe-auth-FKOXYPWa.js";
```

It then exercises four cases against that deployed openclaw bundle:

- A) Local probe, `gateway.auth.mode = "token"`, no credentials configured, `url: http://127.0.0.1:18789` -> expect structured `failureReason`.
- B) Local probe with explicit `auth: { mode: "none" }`, same local URL -> expect no `failureReason` (open gateway is intentional).
- C) Local probe with `auth: { mode: "token" }` and a resolvable env SecretRef token (`PROOF_68280_GATEWAY_TOKEN`) -> expect `auth.token` populated, no `failureReason`.
- D) Remote URL (`https://example.com/gateway`) with `mode: "remote"` and `auth: { mode: "token" }` -> expect fall-through to existing behavior, no `failureReason` from this helper.

Evidence after fix: captured live from the deployed openclaw build on rh-bot.lan and saved to `/tmp/proof-68280-amittell.txt`:

```
PASS: A local-no-creds-token-mode -- sync.failureReason="Missing gateway auth token." async.failureReason="Missing gateway auth token."
PASS: B local-auth-none-open-gateway -- sync.failureReason=undefined async.failureReason=undefined
PASS: C local-token-mode-resolved -- auth.token="resolved-loopback-token-value" failureReason=undefined
PASS: D remote-mode-fallback -- sync.failureReason=undefined async.failureReason=undefined
---
TOTAL: passed=4 failed=0
```

Observed result after fix:

- Local probe with required-auth mode and no credentials -> immediate structured `failureReason` ("Missing gateway auth token."), no doomed WebSocket attempt.
- Local probe with `mode: "none"` -> no `failureReason`; preserves open-gateway operator choice.
- Local probe with `mode: "token"` + resolvable env SecretRef -> credential resolves, no `failureReason`, probe proceeds with auth as before.
- Remote URL target -> no fail-fast, falls through to existing remote probe behavior.

What was not tested: full end-to-end CLI invocation through `openclaw channels status --probe` against an externally restarted gateway on rh-bot (would require deliberately re-keying the live gateway auth on a host in active use). The deployed bundle was exercised directly via its public exported helpers, which is the exact entry point the CLI gather/scan/probe-run paths use. Remote-mode against a real remote gateway was not exercised live; case D proves only that the local fail-fast does not trip in remote mode.
target.author: amittell

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
